title: Hadrolaelia × pilcheri
intro: Hadrolaelia × pilcheri, commonly known as the Pilcher's Hadrolaelia, is a hybrid orchid species belonging to the family Orchidaceae. This plant is valued for its potential health benefits, including its anti-inflammatory and antimicrobial properties, which may aid in the treatment of various conditions such as wounds, infections, and respiratory issues. The bioactive constituents of Hadrolaelia × pilcheri include flavonoids, alkaloids, and phenolic compounds, which are responsible for its medicinal properties. In traditional medicine, Hadrolaelia × pilcheri is often used to prepare teas, infusions, and tinctures, which are commonly employed to treat respiratory problems and to promote wound healing.
taxonomy: Hadrolaelia × pilcheri is classified under the family Orchidaceae and is a hybrid of two species within the same genus. It belongs to the subtribe Pleurothallidinae, which comprises a group of orchids commonly known as the 'pleurothallids'. This species is a result of crossing Hadrolaelia brunnlechnerae with Hadrolaelia parviunguiculata. The classification of Hadrolaelia × pilcheri provides insight into its evolutionary history and genetic makeup. As an orchid hybrid, its classification is essential for understanding its characteristics and behavior.
